texts.<|25.10|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> If someone says, "This is easy," don't trust them.<|6.50|> <|6.50|> Reading, marking, learning, and inwardly digesting is the process <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> and those historically marginalized by various interpretations of Scripture.<|6.34|> <|7.34|> And deciding that we are all best served by contextual, up fear of a pending doomsday.<|24.00|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> but digging deep into the texts, appreciating their original context and audiences, and seeking to interpret crushed by history's most powerful empires, speaking in code languages not understood by the powerful.<|14.00|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Our task is to hear these voices, this literature, these sacred words, and say,<|8.30|> <|8.30|> there is more to this, there must be more to this.<|12.34|> <|13.52|> And leaving our pews, approaching this altar, receiving Christ's body and blood,<|20.50|> <|20.96|> and knowing in our bones, oh, there is certainly more to this.<|26.32|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Thank you.<|1.78|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Thank you.<|7.50|><|endoftext|>
